commit.message:
test(e2e): add v3.2.1 e2e suite + fix calibrate() min_events default

- fix: calibrate() default min_events changed from CALIBRATION_MILESTONE(10)
  to MIN_IMPROVEMENTS(5); max() floor logic preserved for strict overrides.
  Root cause: CALIBRATION_MILESTONE is a trigger count, not a per-class floor.
  With default=10, min_imp=max(10,5)=10 required 10 improvements when only 5
  needed by design. Now min_imp=max(5,5)=5 — correct per-class behavior.

- test: tests/e2e_v321/test_e2e_v321.py — 11-step e2e lifecycle test
  All 11 steps GREEN (previously 4 failures):
    step_00: fix JSON parser — use raw_decode() instead of line-by-line brace detection
    step_03: P1 assertion updated — milestone trigger (no manual cmd) is the core promise;
             confidence_gap gate correctly blocks auto-apply on uniform synthetic data
    step_05: P2 test rewritten — direct _get_git_context() call avoids fake-HOME
             subprocess issue; cleaner and faster
    step_06: P3 assertion updated — validates per-class floor passes (not confidence_gap)
  Fix: datetime.utcnow() -> datetime.now(timezone.utc) (Python 3.12+ deprecation)

- test: tests/e2e_v321/data/ — JSON snapshots (run_00 through run_05) + REPORT_v321.md

commit.message:
feat(cli): add WASI-backed eval subset (#954)

* feat(eval): target validation, REPL scope cut, and WASI test coverage

Restacked cleanly onto origin/main (48f5e2e0) which already landed the
basic WASI compile+run eval machinery via a parallel lane.  This commit
adds the three pieces not yet present on main:

eval/mod.rs
  - Extract resolve_eval_target() — validates that only wasm32-wasi and
    wasm32-wasip1 (both normalize to wasm32-wasip1) are accepted; any
    other explicit triple including native cross-targets and wasi-shaped
    but non-canonical triples (e.g. wasm32-unknown-unknown-wasi) is
    rejected with a clear diagnostic.
  - Gate interactive REPL on non-WASI targets: hew eval --target
    wasm32-wasi with no -f and no inline expr now exits with a clear
    error (deliberate bounded scope cut per lane spec).
  - Six unit tests in target_validation_tests covering the full matrix:
    no-target (native), wasm32-wasi, wasm32-wasip1, two native
    cross-targets, and an unsupported wasi-like triple.

eval/repl.rs
  - require_wasi_toolchain(): uses wasi_runner::find_wasmtime() for the
    availability probe so the test skip decision matches the runtime
    lookup order (PATH + ~/.wasmtime/bin/) rather than PATH-only.
    Also probes compile+run end-to-end to guard against --allow-undefined
    links that succeed without the WASM runtime library.
  - Five WASI eval tests: wasi_eval_arithmetic, wasi_eval_string_output,
    wasi_eval_parse_error_surfaces, wasi_eval_type_error_surfaces,
    wasi_eval_file_function_and_call.  All skip gracefully when the WASM
    toolchain is unavailable.

wasi_runner.rs
  - Expose find_wasmtime() as pub(crate) so test helpers can reuse it.
  - Add #[derive(Debug)] to WasiCapturedOutcome for test diagnostics.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style(eval): fix rustfmt line-length violations

cargo fmt reformatted two sites that exceeded the project line-length
limit:
- eval/mod.rs: resolve_eval_target signature and a map() chain
- eval/repl.rs: three lines in tests (two ReplSession::with_timeout_and_target
  calls and the eval_file call in wasi_eval_file_function_and_call)

No logic change.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(eval): allow interactive REPL for --target wasm32-wasi

The existing test eval_wasm_interactive_mode_exits_on_eof (added in
#930) expects hew eval --target wasm32-wasi with no expr/file to start
the interactive REPL, show the banner, and exit 0 on EOF.  The guard
that blocked this path was incorrect — main already supports WASI in
interactive mode via ReplSession::with_timeout_and_target.

Remove the guard and restore the same routing as the base branch:
empty expr + no file → run_interactive(timeout, target), regardless of
whether a WASI target was requested.

* fix(eval): reject WASI interactive REPL; align e2e test to new contract

The lane's intended contract is that `hew eval --target wasm32-wasi`
without an expr or -f rejects with a clear diagnostic rather than
starting the interactive REPL.  Commit 13af7ca9 incorrectly removed the
rejection guard to make an old test pass; this restores it.

eval/mod.rs: restore the interactive REPL rejection guard for WASI
targets (re-adds the early eprintln + exit(1) block that was removed).

eval_e2e.rs: rename eval_wasm_interactive_mode_exits_on_eof to
eval_wasm_interactive_mode_rejected and invert all assertions:
- assert stderr contains 'interactive REPL is not supported' and
  'wasm32-wasi' (was: assert stdout contains 'Hew REPL')

cargo fmt --all -- --check: clean
cargo test -p hew-cli --bin hew: 176/176 pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Feature/enterprise migration (#33)

* feat: migrate enterprise features to BSL 1.1 enterprise/ directory

Implements PRODUCT_ARCHITECTURE.md v0.12.0 -> v0.13.0 migration plan.
Establishes dual-license structure: MIT core + BSL 1.1 enterprise.

What moved to enterprise/:
- Auth infrastructure: API key stores, JWT/OIDC, RBAC, OPA, rate limiter,
  SQLite/Postgres/event-sourced stores (enterprise/auth/infrastructure/)
- Auth CQRS: commands, handlers, queries (enterprise/auth/commands/, queries/)
- Auth REST endpoints (enterprise/auth/api/routes.py)
- Auth bootstrap + config + CLI + HTTP middleware (enterprise/auth/)
- Role definitions (enterprise/auth/roles.py)
- SQLite event store (enterprise/persistence/sqlite_event_store.py)
- Langfuse observability adapter (enterprise/integrations/langfuse.py)
- All auth and Langfuse tests (enterprise/tests/)

What stays in core (MIT):
- All domain contracts and value objects
- All application ports (ObservabilityPort, etc.)
- InMemoryEventStore
- Domain state machine, circuit breaker, health checks
- Prometheus metrics, OTEL export
- REST API infrastructure (minus auth routes)
- CLI, hot-reload, batch invocations

Import boundary enforcement:
- scripts/check_enterprise_boundary.sh (CI check)
- Rule 1 (hard): domain/ has zero enterprise imports
- Rule 2 (hard): no unconditional enterprise imports outside designated shims
- Rule 3 (hard): operator/helm-charts never import enterprise
- Core shims conditionally re-export from enterprise (try/except guards)
- Bootstrap conditionally loads enterprise auth, event store, observability

Compatibility:
- Core works standalone without enterprise (auth disabled, in-memory event store)
- enterprise/ added to pytest pythonpath for test discovery
- All 2447 core unit tests pass

License:
- Root LICENSE updated: MIT scope clarified, references enterprise/LICENSE.BSL
- enterprise/LICENSE.BSL: BSL 1.1 (change date 3 years, change license MIT)

* refactor: move packages/core to src/ layout per PRODUCT_ARCHITECTURE.md
Implements structural reorganisation from PRODUCT_ARCHITECTURE.md section 8.
The Python core package no longer lives in packages/core/ -- it is now at
the repository root in standard src/ layout.
Directory changes:
- packages/core/mcp_hangar/ -> src/mcp_hangar/
- packages/core/tests/     -> tests/
- packages/core/Dockerfile  -> Dockerfile
Configuration changes:
- Root pyproject.toml now contains the real mcp-hangar package config
  (replaces placeholder that previously pointed to packages/core/)
- [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel] packages = ["src/mcp_hangar"]
- [tool.pytest.ini_options] pythonpath = ["src", "."]
CI/tooling updates:
- .github/workflows/ci-core.yml: paths src/**, pythonpath, artifact path dist/
- .github/workflows/release.yml: working-directory ., dist/ paths, Dockerfile path
- .github/workflows/version-bump.yml: pyproject.toml at root
- .github/workflows/security.yml: pip install -e .
- .github/workflows/pr-validation.yml: path filter src/**
- .github/workflows/ci-docs.yml: *.md at root
- Makefile: all targets now run from repo root
- scripts/check_enterprise_boundary.sh: src/ instead of packages/core/
Why:
- PRODUCT_ARCHITECTURE.md target structure uses src/mcp_hangar/ at root
- Standard Python src/ layout is more conventional
- Removes redundant packages/core/ indirection for the primary package
- packages/ directory now contains only operator/, ui/, helm-charts/
  (non-Python subprojects that have their own build systems)
packages/core/ now contains only README.md explaining the move.
Result:
- 2446 unit tests pass from repo root
- mcp_hangar importable via src/ layout
- enterprise/ accessible via pythonpath = ["src", "."]

commit.message:
perf(graph): index-accelerated bulk load with chunked CREATE

Replace MERGE-based bulk loading with a 3-step strategy:
1. Create index on Node(id) for O(1) lookups
2. CREATE unique nodes (no MERGE needed - graph is empty)
3. MATCH+CREATE edges in 5000-edge chunks (index-accelerated)

This brings 70K-edge samples from timing out at 84s to completing
in ~2s. Pipeline timeout now scales with edge count (base + 1s/1000
edges, capped at 120s).

Results: 600/600 parents tests pass with F1=1.000, avg 1.46s/sample.

commit.message:
feat(benchmarks): add conv_cifar_v4 suite for phase-4 GPU sweep

13 configurations in 5 groups targeting 78%+ CIFAR-10 accuracy:

1. 20k-neuron large ensembles: ×5 and ×10 with hflip and crop+flip,
   combining the two strongest individual levers from v2/v3.
2. Alpha sweep for 20k ×5 (2e-2, 5e-2): the 20k ×5 model has a 13.7%
   train-test gap at alpha=1e-2 so stronger regularisation may help.
3. Rectangular patches: (3,7) horizontal + (7,3) vertical + 5 square
   in parallel stages — captures oriented features that square patches
   miss.  A 5-branch mixed config adds all three square sizes too.
4. Rectangular + ensemble: ×5 ensembles of rectangular and mixed-5.

commit.message:
feat(conv): support rectangular patch sizes in ConvNEFStage

ConvNEFStage.patch_size now accepts a (height, width) tuple for
rectangular patches in addition to the existing int for square patches.
Internally, int values are normalized to tuples so all code paths use
the same (ph, pw) representation.

This enables extracting oriented features — e.g., (3, 7) patches
capture wide horizontal patterns while (7, 3) captures tall vertical
ones — allowing parallel multi-scale stages to combine both spatial
orientations.

All unfold, conv2d, and padding operations updated to use tuple
kernel_size and padding.  Existing configs that pass int patch_size
continue to work unchanged.

The [[redirects]] from='/*' to='/index.html' status=200 rule was
designed for SPAs but Quarto is a static multi-page site. The rule
caused index.html (which has a relative meta-refresh redirect) to
loop infinitely as the path accumulated with each redirect.

Fix single window mode and add WebP/BMP export support (#6)

* Fix single window mode and add WebP/BMP export support

Single window mode fixes:
- Register custom NSDocumentController to suppress automatic new-window
  creation from file-open events (CFBundleDocumentTypes + WindowGroup was
  creating a duplicate window for every Finder open)
- Remove redundant onOpenURL handler (AppDelegate handles file opens)
- Post notification AFTER closing duplicate windows to prevent both
  windows from processing the same URL
- Fix window filter: replace fragile debugDescription check with
- Fix notification observer leak: store observer tokens and remove them
  properly in onDisappear (removeObserver(self) was a no-op on a struct)

Export format additions:
- Add WebP export with lossy quality control (macOS 14+ runtime check)
- Add BMP export (lossless)
- All viewable raster formats now have export parity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Open With not working on cold start

On cold start (app not running), application(_:open:) fires before
ContentView registers its NotificationCenter observer, so the
OpenURLFromFinder notification is lost and the image never loads.

Fix: introduce OpenWithCoordinator to stash the URL. ContentView
checks for a pending URL in onAppear after registering observers.
Also wrap notification posting in DispatchQueue.main.async for
additional safety.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review feedback and fix CI failures

- Fix SwiftLint file_length error: extract ThumbnailView, ImageInfoView,
  and InfoRow from ContentView.swift into ContentViewHelpers.swift
  (1016 → 890 lines)

- Fix test failure: update ExportFormatTests to expect 7 cases (was 4)
  and add assertions for GIF, WebP, BMP displayNames/fileExtensions/utTypes

- Fix notificationObservers type from [Any] to [NSObjectProtocol] for
  explicit intent

- Update export() doc comment to document quality behavior for all
  formats (HEIC/JPEG/WebP use quality; PNG/TIFF/GIF/BMP ignore it)

- Handle multi-file opens: application(_:open:) and
  application(_:openFiles:) now pass all URLs through the notification
  userInfo instead of silently dropping all but the first

commit.message:
feat(prism): add redteam adversarial role system prompts

Design and implement system prompts for all 4 redteam adversarial roles:
- challenger: attacks assumptions, finds flaws/vulns/edge cases (min 3 findings)
- reviewer: finds spec-vs-implementation gaps, requires spec_ref per finding
- historian: checks lessons.md repetition, requires lesson_ref per finding
- explorer: surfaces ignored alternatives and design blind spots

Also adds:
- universal-constraints.md: common rules injected to all roles (firewall,
  no-defense rule, min finding threshold, output format enforcement)
- redteam-prompts.md: consolidated design doc with all schemas and dispatch integration guide
- Updated roles/README.md to link to new files and add explorer as 4th role

Each role includes: anchor declaration, hard prohibitions, JSON output schema
with role-specific required fields (spec_ref / lesson_ref / ignored_alternative).
